mechanism_narrative: Higher 3-month growth in retail sales signals accelerating household demand and near-term revenue momentum for listed firms, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth does not produce higher 20-day S&P 500 forward returns in walk-forward tests, or if Rank IC is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is above 4.5% and rising over 3 months, tighter monetary conditions raise discount rates and restrain credit creation, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if mean triggered returns exceed non-triggered returns with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous payroll slowdown and unemployment increase signals deteriorating labor income and rising recession risk, reducing expected earnings and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change falls below its 12-month average and UNRATE rises at least 0.2pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS slowdown and UNRATE rise signal fails to underperform unconditional 60-day returns by at least 1 percentage point on average in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: An upside crossover in industrial production indicates improving factory output and operating leverage, which lifts near-term earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO crosses above its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO upside crossovers are followed by non-positive average 20-day S&P 500 returns, or if hit rate is not above 52% after transaction-free walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts signals weakening residential investment, construction employment, and durable-goods demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% over 6 months
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST drawdowns greater than 10% over 6 months are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation accelerates while the federal funds rate is already high, equity valuations compress because investors price tighter policy and lower real margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth rises and FEDFUNDS > 4.0%
failure_condition: Reject if the joint CPIAUCSL acceleration and high FEDFUNDS condition has average 20-day returns statistically indistinguishable from all other observations at p > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A falling unemployment rate signals improving household income security and reduced recession probability, increasing risk appetite and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls at least 0.2pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE declines do not deliver a higher 5-day S&P 500 hit rate than the unconditional baseline, or if the excess hit rate is below 2 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Positive real GDP growth combined with rising retail sales confirms broad demand expansion rather than inventory accumulation, supporting earnings revisions and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth > 0 and RSAFS 3-month growth > 0
failure_condition: Reject if the joint positive GDPC1 and RSAFS condition fails to outperform unconditional 60-day S&P 500 returns by at least 1 percentage point on average in walk-forward tests.
